text: Bruno Fernandes (137 GA) surpasses Eden Hazard (136 GA) in Premier League goal contributions — doing it in 22 fewer games.

Mohamed Salah has scored or assisted against every side he has played multiple times against in Liverpool's Premier League history:
⚽🅰️ 20 G/A vs Newcastle
⚽🅰️ 19 G/A vs Man Utd
⚽🅰️ 17 G/A vs Brighton
⚽🅰️ 17 G/A vs West Ham
⚽🅰️ 16 G/A vs Tottenham
Dominik Szoboszlai and Florian Wirtz are the only players to complete 400+ final third passes in the Premier League this season.
Noni Madueke in the Premier League since January 25, 2025: 25 matches, 0 goals, 0 assists.
Against Crystal Palace, Andrey Santos became the first Chelsea midfielder in the last 10 seasons to record 3+ tackles, interceptions, clearances, and fouls won in a single Premier League game.

Newcastle's pass completion rate in the first half against Wolves (94%) was the highest by any team in a half of Premier League football without registering a shot on target — on record since 2003/04.

Aerial duel win rate in the Premier League this season:
Virgil van Dijk - 75.1%
Tosin Adarabioyo - 68.1%
Malick Thiaw - 65.3%
Boubacar Kamara - 65.2%
Cristian Romero - 65.1%
Only 3 players in Premier League history have scored 20+ goals in 3 consecutive seasons. Salah just joined them.
Trent Alexander-Arnold averages more key passes per 90 than any other defender in the top 5 leagues. It's not even close.
Vinicius Jr has attempted 4.3 dribbles per game this season — and completed 68% of them. No winger in La Liga comes close.
Atletico Madrid have conceded just 3 goals from open play at home all season. The Metropolitano has become a fortress again.
Yamal is 17 and already averaging 2.1 shot-creating actions per 90 in La Liga. At his age, Messi was still in the B team.
Bayern have scored 40% of their Bundesliga goals in the second half. Their opponents have scored 60% of goals against them in the first half. Make of that what you will.
Harry Kane is averaging 0.91 non-penalty goals per 90 in the Bundesliga this season. That's not a striker having a good run — that's a generational output.
Dortmund have had more shots from outside the box than any team in Germany this season. They've scored 2. Someone tell them.
Inter's defensive record at home this season: 18 games, 4 goals conceded. That's a fortress, not a football club.
Osimhen has scored 14 Serie A goals but only 3 have come from inside the six-yard box. He's a threat from anywhere in the area.
Roma have been involved in more games decided by a single goal than any other Serie A side. Either very good or very lucky — the xG says it's somewhere in between.
